paper_title: Algal Culture and Statistical Analysis Methods

methods.description: This method describes the process for obtaining, identifying, rendering axenic, and maintaining cultures of the polar Thalassiosira gravida and temperate Thalassiosira rotula strains under controlled climate conditions for experimental use.

steps.description: The polar Thalassiosira gravida (central Arctic Ocean) was obtained from the Norwegian culture collection of algae (NORCCA strain number UIO 478).The temperate Thalassiosira rotula from the German bight, was provided by the Harder Lab (University of Bremen; strain T. rotula_S16).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain specific algal strains from culture collections

steps.description: Both strains were identified by their ITS1 sequences (electronic supplementary material, figure S1)
steps.searchable_summary: identify algal strains using ITS1 sequencing

steps.description: an axenic culture was rendered following the protocol of Mönnich et al. [15].

Axenicity of a culture was referred to as continuous absence of any contaminants stained with SYBR Green by regular (i.e.every 5-7 days) epifluorescence microscopy at 400× magnification (electronic supplementary material, figure S2).
steps.searchable_summary: verify axenicity of cultures using SYBR Green staining and epifluorescence microscopy

steps.description: All cultures were maintained in climate chambers at a photoperiod of 16 : 8 h, a light intensity of 50 µmol photons m −2 s −1 and a temperature of 4°C and 15°C for the polar and temperate strains, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain algal cultures in climate chambers under specific light and temperature conditions
inputs: 50 µmol photons m −2 s −1 light intensity
outputs: algal cultures maintained under controlled conditions

steps.description: Cultures were grown in filter-sterilized artificial seawater medium (ESAW) containing 1/5 of the vitamin concentration proposed by Harrison et al. [16]
steps.searchable_summary: grow algal cultures in modified ESAW medium
inputs: modified vitamin concentration (1/5 of Harrison et al. [16])
outputs: algal cultures growing in specific medium

steps.description: and kept in exponential growth by semi-continuous dilution.
steps.searchable_summary: maintain cultures in exponential growth phase using semi-continuous dilution

methods.description: This method details the statistical procedures used to analyze the maximum growth rates (µ max) of the algal strains, including data cleaning, model fitting, ANOVA, post-hoc tests, and effect size calculation.

steps.description: After blank values of fluorescence in the growth medium were subtracted from the raw experimental fluorescence data, maximum growth rates (µ max ) were calculated for each experimental unit by fitting nonlinear models to the data using the 'growthrates' package [17].
steps.searchable_summary: subtract blank fluorescence values and calculate maximum growth rates by fitting nonlinear models using R package 'growthrates'
outputs: maximum growth rates (µ max) for each experimental unit

steps.description: To test for main and interactive effects, a three-way ANOVA (type III SS) was conducted for T. gravida and T. rotula, respectively, with µ max as dependent, and temperature, photoperiod and the presence/absence of the native microbiome as independent variables.
steps.searchable_summary: perform three-way ANOVA (Type III SS) on maximum growth rates with temperature, photoperiod, and microbiome as independent variables

steps.description: Groups were weighted by the inverse of their variance to account for heteroscedasticity.
steps.searchable_summary: apply inverse variance weighting to groups to address heteroscedasticity

steps.description: Post-hoc analyses were conducted with Games-Howell tests.

The effect sizes of the main effects and the three-way interaction were calculated with the 'variancePartition' package [18] which reports the fraction of explained variance to be attributed to each variable while correcting for all other variables.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate effect sizes (fraction of explained variance) using R package 'variancePartition'

steps.description: All statistical analyses and graphs were performed with the R environment 4.2.2 [19].
steps.searchable_summary: perform all statistical analyses and generate graphs using R environment
